title: taskwrite.py board mutation is a lock-free read-modify-write — concurrent Claude+Codex sessions in one repo lose updates to open-tasks.md
evidence: main(): `text = tb.read_board(board_path)` … transform … mkstemp + `os.replace(tmp_path, board_path)`. The replace is crash-atomic but there is no lock, no re-read, and no compare-and-swap between the read at line 75 and the replace at line 114. This branch makes two hosts (ca on Claude Code, ca-codex on Codex CLI) first-class writers of the SAME .codearbiter/open-tasks.md; two sessions running /ca:task concurrently interleave read→write and the second os.replace silently discards the first session's add/start/done (including a minted dotted ID / next_seq collision — both sessions mint the same seq).
impact: Silent lost updates on the task board: a task marked done reverts to in-progress, or two tasks receive the same dotted ID, with no error on either side. Same pattern applies to any concurrent same-host pair, but dual-host sharing is the branch's headline configuration and doubles exposure.
recommendation: Add optimistic concurrency to the write: re-read the board immediately before os.replace and abort with a retry message if it changed since the initial read (mirroring heal_statusline_wiring's reliability-009 pattern), or take an O_CREAT|O_EXCL sidecar lock around the RMW.
acceptance_criteria: Two interleaved taskwrite invocations (read1, read2, write1, write2) never silently drop write1 — the second either merges or fails loudly with a retry instruction
